skills.frame-analogize: Build and stress-test an analogy for an already-understood concept. Use for ELI5, plain-language, metaphor, or 'explain differently' requests; compares candidates, names where the winner breaks.
skills.frame-dialectic: Put a forming claim on trial: steelman both sides, expose the load-bearing assumption, and name a deciding experiment. Use for devil's advocate, strongest case for/against, or stress-testing a thesis.
skills.frame-first-principles: Rebuild a question or decision from irreducible axioms, separating necessity from convention. Use for first-principles reasoning, challenging assumptions, or rebuilding an approach from scratch.
skills.frame-graft: Design by mapping every primitive of a mature donor model onto a new problem, classifying fit, break, and adapt. Use when reasoning through another system's structure or mapping X onto Y.
skills.frame-orthogonal: Factor a tangled problem into independent axes and verify that changing one does not move the others. Use to disentangle concerns, test orthogonality, or expose conflated dimensions.
skills.nav-audit: Read-only codebase architecture audit against deep-module principles. Use for health reviews, architectural smells, feature-readiness checks, or thorough domain sweeps; reports findings, no code change.
skills.nav-compose: Author or restructure prose as a navigable deep module: lead with the point, group by knowledge, keep each fact owned once. Use for ADRs, specs, READMEs, design docs, or tightening structure.
skills.nav-do: Implement a small, decided, behavior-changing code change without a plan artifact. Use for quick features or small fixes; not for behavior-preserving refactors or large/spec-ambiguous work.
skills.nav-map: Render or refresh a bilingual repo-level codebase map from load-bearing files and headers. Use for architecture maps, module maps, or refreshing docs/codebase-map/index.html. Onboarding chat: nav-tour.
skills.nav-plan: Turn a feature spec or change intent into a codebase-grounded implementation plan after auditing touched domains and resolving ambiguity. Writes only the final plan artifact with location consent.
skills.nav-refactor: Execute a behavior-preserving structural refactor using verbatim moves, test gates, and real-app verification. Use to extract, split, decompose, or reorganize code without changing behavior.
skills.nav-sync: Refresh load-bearing files' top headers so their first lines accurately describe purpose, details, and dependencies. Use to add, standardize, or repair file headers after code changes.
skills.nav-tour: Guide a conversational walkthrough of what a codebase does, how it works, and why (rationale labeled Recorded/Inferred/Unknown); ends by proposing a shared model for the user to correct. Read-only, in-chat; not a map render or catchup.
skills.reflect-catchup: Reconstruct the current state of work from git, diffs, files, plans, and live context. Use for 'where are we?', resuming after a break, or getting oriented; read-only, present-state only.
skills.reflect-observe: Surface this session's candidate durable learnings, let the user choose, then write only selected observations or skill feedback. Use to record insights or decide what is worth preserving.
skills.reflect-park: Write the session's cursor (goal, done, now, open, next, plus the current git SHA) into HANDOFF.md before stepping away, overwriting any prior file. Use to hand off work; explicitly invoked on Codex.
skills.relay-digest: Read a relay coordination repo and show unresolved thoughts that mention the current person. Use for relay catch-up, pending reviews, or 'what needs me?'; read-only and explicitly invoked on Codex.
skills.relay-format: Validate and normalize one relay project's thought frontmatter without changing meaning. Use to lint thoughts, fix metadata, or migrate old relay entries to the current format; diff-gated.
skills.relay-launch: Scaffold a relay coordination project and roster, or add a person and assign their role. Use to create a relay project, register, onboard, or role-assign a teammate; writes relay.yml/project.yml behind a diff gate.
skills.relay-report: Append a progress or framing thought to a relay coordination repo so a counterpart can review it. Use to post relay updates or alignment notes; writes only in the relay content repo, diff-gated.
skills.relay-review: Respond to a relay thought with acknowledgment, agreement, comment, or requested change by appending a linked thought. Use for relay review; writes one diff-gated response.
skills.relay-settle: Distill agreed relay thoughts into the append-only decision ledger and regenerate the active decisions view. Use to settle, crystallize, or log project decisions; never deletes or moves thoughts.
skills.research-critique: Adversarially review an argument document — paper, RFC, design proposal, ADR: test claims against evidence, locate real sources of gains, and self-attack criticisms. Use for referee reports or evidence reviews.
skills.research-dissect: Break an argument document — paper, RFC, blog post, design proposal, ADR — into Gap, Claim, Mechanism, Evidence, and Conclusion. Also verifies cited-as versus actually-says for individual citations.
skills.research-provenance: Audit citations in your own document (paper, RFC, ADR) by tracing load-bearing claims, numbers, and quotes to verified sources; classify first-hand, second-hand, or orphaned.
skills.research-untangle: Map relationships across multiple argument documents (papers, RFCs, design proposals): lineage, clusters, contradictions, contested ground, and optionally where a claim fits. Use for literature landscapes and related-work positioning.
skills.shape-align: Decide with the user what is In progress, Next, and Future from current decisions and actual work, then maintain a lean plan.md board. Use to align priorities or refresh what comes next.
skills.shape-build: Drive a blueprints plan's In-progress items to verified completion, serially by default, with planning, scoped execution, checks, and board updates. Use to implement or ship the current plan.
skills.shape-dogfood: Use a completed feature like a real user via browser or CLI, record evidence, and identify workflow friction or missing intent coverage. Use when a built feature feels awkward or unsmooth.
skills.shape-elicit: Converge a conceptual decision or root cause by grilling the user to a choice; diagnoses stalls, offering /shape:survey or /shape:probe. Use for undecided concepts or logic holes, not visuals.
skills.shape-mockup: Converge a visual, structural, flow, or option decision by generating a real interactive HTML artifact. Use to mock, compare, diagram, visualize, or try variants; never substitutes prose or ASCII.
skills.shape-position: Author and maintain a project's ratified core principle documents from messy, repeated inputs. Use to establish positioning, graduate durable thoughts, or repair accreted canon, gated edits.
skills.shape-probe: Design and run a minimal experiment (A/B, blind judgment test, or behavior probe) when a fork can't be settled by argument. Use to test an undecided claim, not for preference or friction.
skills.shape-reconcile: Compare blueprints thoughts, plans, and mockups with shipped reality, then propose per-file amend, consolidate, graduate, retire, or prune actions. Read-only until each write is approved.
skills.shape-setup: Scaffold a new project to a verified running baseline using an archetype or grounded exemplar, with staged commits and verification. Use to start repos or projects, not to add features.
skills.shape-survey: Map a decision space into independent axes grounded in the real repo, then report the diff versus what the user already knows. Use before deciding in unfamiliar terrain, not for repo maps or research.
